channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Fed meeting live updates: Here's what to watch ahead of Kevin Warsh's first rate decision
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s rate decision on Wednesday marks the first one with Kevin Warsh at the helm.

item.title: Watch: Trump speaks at G7 summit after threatening Iran
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday at the G7 conference that the U.S. will "go right back to dropping bombs" if he doesn't like the Iran deal.

item.title: SpaceX stock sinks 5%, losing momentum after a multiday rally
item.description: Elon Musk's space and AI company has seen its stock surge since its blockbuster IPO on Friday.

item.title: Trump sabotages Senate bid to fast-track Clayton as DNI
item.description: Any delay, and continuation of acting DNI Bill Pulte in the role, is likely to endanger the reauthorization of a key U.S. intelligence authority.

item.title: Amazon AI exec predicts first 'commercially useful' quantum computers in 5-7 years
item.description: Quantum computing is becoming an increasingly competitive field with tech giants like Microsoft, Google and IBM developing the technology.

item.title: This medical diagnostics stock is ready for a big move higher, charts show
item.description: Frank Cappelleri of CappThesis breaks down the technicals on NTRA.

item.title: Allbirds continues AI pivot with name change and CEO hire, sending stock soaring
item.description: Allbirds became NewBird AI in April and said it would trade shoes for AI compute infrastructure.

item.title: Less than one-third of global workers feel their jobs are safe, ADP survey data shows
item.description: Even with global unemployment at historically low levels, workers are not feeling secure.

item.title: June Monthly Meeting: Jim Cramer looks at SpaceX's impact on the market and our stocks
item.description: There will be no Morning Meeting on June 17. Join Jim Cramer for the June Monthly Meeting on June 17 at 12pm ET.Jim and Jeff Marks, director of Club portfolio analysis, will also provide their updated views on all 34 names in the CNBC Investing Club portfolio and take member questions.

item.title: Carvana’s new vehicle strategy turns dealership into ‘playground,’ test-drive center with sales all online
item.description: But don't expect Carvana to sell you a vehicle at one of its seven Stellantis stores, marking a stark contrast from typical franchised dealers.

item.title: 'Passive' investors who dodged bitcoin are now forced to own SpaceX, which is three times more volatile
item.description: Many advisors and money-managers invested in index funds who will soon be owners of Elon Musk's astronomic ambitions

item.title: The private space race is spurring a luxury hotel land grab for Florida oceanfront property
item.description: The rise of the private space industry, with companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin, has spurred demand for commercial real estate on Florida's space coast.

item.title: Target, Walmart and Amazon among brands losing LGBTQ+ consumer spending, new survey says
item.description: LGBTQ+ consumers are shifting spending toward companies they view as supporting DEI and away from those seen as retreating, HRC survey finds.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz threat level downgraded after Iran deal, says U.S.-led maritime security group
item.description: The behavior of Iranian forces "has become less volatile" and the U.S. Navy continues to provide oversight, the Joint Maritime Information Center said.

item.title: SpaceX surge is creating a unique hedging opportunity
item.description: Tuesday's launch of SpaceX options set new records for a first-day, post-IPO options volume.

item.title: The broadening stock market trade: What to buy outside AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been the driving force for the stock market since late 2022, with investors looking for exposure to the emerging technology.

item.title: CNBC to wade further into live sports with 11 simulcast WNBA games
item.description: The simulcasts will expose the WNBA to an affluent audience on CNBC and marks a new chapter for the business news network after it spun out from Comcast.

item.title: From supply shock to oil glut: IEA flags scale of demand destruction caused by Iran war
item.description: The energy supply shock triggered by the Iran War has prompted the IEA to slash its global oil demand forecast.

item.title: CME Group's Terry Duffy to step down in 2027, CFO Lynne Fitzpatrick to become CEO
item.description: Duffy, 67, will transition to executive chairman effective March 1, 2027, the company said Wednesday.

item.title: Anthropic asked for regulation. Washington went much further
item.description: Anthropic has long been supportive of AI regulation, but the Trump administration is coming down particularly hard on the AI company.

item.title: 3 of our stocks striking gold and 3 others hitting the skids over the past 3 weeks
item.description: It was a volatile period for stocks. The S&P 500 saw its worst session in eight months, just days after the index reached record highs.

item.title: As federal aid for college shrinks, here’s what to look for in a private student loan
item.description: With new federal loan caps taking effect on July 1, more families will need to rely on private lenders to pay for college. Here's how to make the best choice.

item.title: Abercrombie & Fitch begins selling third-party shoe brands in latest bid to chase growth
item.description: Abercrombie & Fitch's explosive growth has slowed, leading the apparel retailer to expand into new categories to boost sales

item.title: Mortgage rates are now falling but demand is still weaker
item.description: A mixed week for mortgage rates resulted in less demand from both current homeowners and potential homebuyers.

item.title: BMW stock slumps to 5-year low as Iran war and China slowdown spark profit warning
item.description: BMW blamed a slowdown in its Chinese market, as well as disruption from the Iran war, as two reasons for the slashed guidance.

item.title: Rivian laying off hundreds of workers amid R2 launch
item.description: Rivian said Tuesday it was laying off hundreds of workers, or less than 2% of its workforce.

item.title: Three Iranian tankers exit U.S. blockade for first time in months as shipowners eye Hormuz in 'wary disbelief'
item.description: At least three Iranian tankers carrying nearly five million barrels of crude oil have exited the U.S. Navy blockade.

item.title: 'A signal of where power sits': Trump and world leaders joined by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google at G7
item.description: Frontier AI risks, infrastructure and sovereignty are all expected to be discussed at the world leaders' summit.

item.title: Intel begins production of most-advanced chip, inching closer to possible Apple deal
item.description: Intel is entering production of 18A-P, its most-advanced chip node that could be the target of a coming deal with Apple

item.title: How Roku fits into Fox's future — and what investors are missing about the deal
item.description: Fox's stock dropped following its announcement that it will acquire Roku for $22 billion. Analysts still think it's a good deal.
